campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. The council also advanced plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including approving a feasibility study and applying for grant funding to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth recreation scholarships
New bike/pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application submitted to fund the Hamilton Avenue crossing project
Interim ordinance #2326 extended (urgency measure)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their opinions on a new city rule. After listening to public input, the council moved forward by introducing a new ordinance and skipping the standard first reading process to speed things up. The council planned to vote on and approve the ordinance at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for final approval at next council meeting
Expedited process used to move ordinance forward

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026 to approve records from several meetings held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused mainly on administrative housekeeping items like approving meeting minutes from various March sessions.
keyTopics: Approved meeting minutes from multiple March sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Administrative/procedural matters

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several updates and agreements affecting city operations. Key discussions included improvements to the airport's baggage system, protections for affordable housing if subsidy contracts end, upgrades to the storm sewer system planning, and progress on a family camp project funded by FEMA disaster relief.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system upgrade contract
Affordable housing protections if funding runs out
Storm sewer system master planning update
FEMA family camp project developments
Review of previous committee meeting minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council met to review Mountain View's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on what construction projects and infrastructure improvements the city will invest in. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in Mountain View.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and which projects will get funding
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
City infrastructure and construction planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council met on April 7, 2026, to conduct regular city business. The meeting included special recognition of Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month, highlighting the city's commitment to celebrating community diversity and ensuring fair housing practices for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular city council business
Community diversity celebration

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends taxpayer money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby city officials to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget and spending visibility
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under municipal code
Public engagement on municipal finances

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to sue or challenge it in court, you have 90 days from the date the decision is finalized to file your case.
keyTopics: Legal challenge deadline: 90 days to file a lawsuit against city decisions
Applies to quasi-adjudicative decisions (hearings, permit denials, appeals)
Governed by California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6
Missing agenda details from provided document excerpt

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including reviewing a major update to the city's five-year community development plan and approving council meeting minutes. They also discussed a potential change to how the city fills council vacancies and reviewed requests from individual council members for future agenda items.
keyTopics: Update to the city's 2025-2030 community development and housing plan
Changes to council vacancy procedures
New agenda item requests from council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner wants to upgrade the building's historic designation and get permission to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space in exchange for making seismic and historic preservation improvements to the building.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Downtown Commercial zone development approval
March 12, 2026 meeting minutes approved
